steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is 'Louisiana resident Oscar James Dunn,' separated from the verb by a long appositive phrase. The main clause needs a finite (tensed) verb to be grammatically complete.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘to begin’: an infinitive, not a finite verb — cannot serve as the main verb of a clause. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘having begun’: a participial phrase, not a finite verb. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘beginning’: a present participle, not a finite verb. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘began’: a finite past-tense verb that can anchor the main clause. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
